briefdescription.para: The following constants map to the XPAR parameters created in the xparameters.h file.

detaileddescription.para: They are defined here such that a user can easily change all the needed parameters in one place. User has to specify a 2MB memory space for filling the frame data. This constant has to be updated based on the memory map of the system.

briefdescription.para: Start and End point Coordinates for the line.
Write a string of characters to the TFT.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTft instance.
is a pointer to the character array to be written to the TFT screen.

memberdef.argsstring: (XTft *InstancePtr, u32 ColStartPos, u32 RowStartPos, u32 ColEndPos, u32 RowEndPos, u32 PixelVal)
memberdef.name: TftDrawLine

briefdescription.para: Draws a line between two points with a specified color.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTft instance.
is the Start point of Column. The valid value is 0 to (XTFT_DISPLAY_WIDTH - 1).
is the Start point of Row. The valid value is 0 to (XTFT_DISPLAY_HEIGHT - 1).
is the End point of Column. The valid value is 0 to (XTFT_DISPLAY_WIDTH - 1).
is the End point of Row. The valid value is 0 to (XTFT_DISPLAY_HEIGHT - 1).
is the Color Value to be put at pixel.

briefdescription.para: This is the example function which performs the following operations on the TFT device -.

listitem.para: Set the color values of foreground and background
Write two characters (S) one after another
Draw a line and scroll the screen once
Disable the display (The screen goes blank)
Scroll the screen once and draw a line

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: Main function that invokes the Tft example.
This file contains a design example using the driver functions of the XTft driver.

detaileddescription.para:
This example shows the usage of the driver/device to
Write a character and write a string of characters
Draw a line
Turn ON/OFF the TFT Device
TFT_FRAME_ADDR specifies the starting address of the 2MB space for storing the frame data and has to be defined by the user based on the system memory map.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a sg 03/24/08 First release 1.00a sg 09/24/08 Updated the example to update the Video Memory Base Address with the Memory specified by the application 2.00a ktn 07/09/09 Updated the example to poll the Vsync(Video address latch) status bit before writing to the Address Register (AR) 4.00a bss 01/22/13 Updated the example to use DDR_HIGH_ADDR to support for some AXI memory controllers, User needs to define it with a valid address. 6.00 ms 01/23/17 Added xil_printf statement in main function to ensure that "Successfully ran" and "Failed" strings are available in all examples. This is a fix for CR-965028. 6.4 sd 07/08/23 Added SDT support. 6.6 sd 12/16/24 Update the DDR_HIGH_ADDR to support BRAM.. 6.8 vlt 12/18/25 Update Doxygen comments to include SDT flow details. 6.8 vlt 01/06/26 fixed GCC warnings.
